item.content:encoded: Got to +100! I'd like the thank the Academy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I laughed at Experiment Log 4164-48 more than I should have, it just caught me off guard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You were actually spot on with most of these! This SCP is the second entry in my Pennsylvania Dark series, about a secretive cult in rural Pennsylvania called the Nachtkarich — but they're not a doomsday cult. They have access to some rudimentary occult techniques, mostly around long-distance telepathy and information transfer, but their primary weapons are lots and lots of guns and home-made pipe bombs. They're fanatics, ready to fight to the deaths of every man, woman, and child in their village against the evils of the Foundation/United States government/any authority figure whom their leader tells them to hate.

They're aware of the Foundation, and allowed the Instruction Manual (of which this is, indeed, the only copy) to fall into the Foundation's hands. They're using it as a kind of psychological warfare, sending the Foundation on a wild goose chase after fictional copies of an instruction manual for a fictional dark god.

The kill count is made up, as well, and is an attempt to make the Foundation fearful and uncertain. The only thing that was real is that they hacked a Foundation computer terminal, and killed a Foundation employee in a drive-by shooting.

I'm curious to know what plot thread you were imagining?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was slightly disappointed with the ending, but only because when it started directly referencing the Foundation, I thought it was going in a much different direction. And that (imagined) plot thread never paid off.

My thought was:

What if there's no actual doomsday cult out there trying to immanentize "����"?

What if there's only one manual, and the Foundation already has it?

What if the text on the document is a total red herring, and the reason the kill count keeps going up so rapidly is because every guard killed in a containment breach, every terminated D-class, and every experimental casualty happening within a certain radius of the manual counts towards the total?

OP is welcome to use or modify this idea if they see fit. ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the number goes down every time someone dies after properly using the document.
Given it speaks of sacrifices before the night of [BLANK].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hilarious. Also, 8/7/18 was my birthday!! Does that mean the 🔷🔷🔷🔷 guy/thing has "arrived"?also, Everytime something touches the document that makes it change, the number goes down?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No I get you. Ratings don't always reflect a work's quality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, to be clear I’m not mad about this, I really do find it funny!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! I’m particularity proud of that one as well. This is my favorite SCP that I’ve written so far, and I legit think it’s kinda funny that it’s also my lowest-rated so far. ¯\_(ツ)_/¯

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think this is remarkably clever, well-written, and creepy as hell. I collect sentences and phrases that I think are well constructed, and I picked out a few from this article. My favorite is below

wherein you should endeavor to squirrel away your device in the south-west corner,

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The "G" in "Jefferson G" stands for "Glattfelder".

EDIT Jan 1st 2019: Oh my goodness, I'm in the Reviewers' Spotlight! Thank you to literally every single person on this website, with special thanks to Zyn and the entire Butterfly Squad for their hard work and dedication to the Foundation, to scpcrnp for the nomination, and to fieldstone and GentleGifts for their help workshopping this article in the Forum.

If you enjoyed this article, check out my other works in the SCP Foundation. I'm particularly proud of SCP-4170, The Dark, and of SCP-4163, The Tetris Prodigy.
